steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main topic — the one idea the entire passage is about. Every sentence should connect to it. The passage opens by announcing the discovery of a runestone, gives its approximate date, and closes by noting its scholarly value for understanding runic alphabets.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘A new method for dating rock samples’: Carbon dating is mentioned only as a means of establishing the stone's age — it is a supporting detail, not the focus. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘A runestone found in Norway’: Every sentence in the passage describes this runestone — its discovery, its age, its scholarly significance. This is the central topic. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The research interests of Kristel Zilmer’: Zilmer appears in one sentence as a source who comments on the stone; the passage is not about her research program. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Battles of the Iron Age’: Iron Age Scandinavia is mentioned only as a historical context for runic alphabets; battles are never discussed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
